raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/2023, I booked a house in XXXX XXXX XXXX for a trip in XXXX. I paid {$4900.00} on my Citibank Visa. I received a notification from XXXX the following day that my booking had been canceled by the owner. I contacted the owner by text message to the number that was provided on the XXXX website. The owner stated that it was a mistake and the listing had not been canceled. I sent them a screenshot showing the listing on my end being canceled and they said they would contact booking and find out what was going on. we both contacted XXXX and they said that they pulled their listing because of the fees with XXXX being too high but I could book privately with them and avoid those fees. I said I would be happy to do that but I would need my refund first and they said they would start the process and I would have my refund within seven days. I stayed in contact with them for the next several weeks waiting for my refund that never came and at one point after not speaking with me for a week or so the person at that phone number claimed they didnt know who I was. I gave all this information to Citibank Visa, along with the text messages that verify this information. Citibank Visa notified me yesterday when I called them to find out the status of my dispute that it was denied because I couldnt produce a credit slip showing the transaction. I explained to them if the merchant was committing fraud how would I be able to get a credit slip? They said they only way they could move forward is if I was able to produce that.
